title: Ai Trading experience
author: Pale-Cap1820
selftext:
I’ve been spending a lot of time trying to automate parts of my trading process, and I wanted to share how it’s gone so far.

I originally started experimenting in crypto on paper because I liked the idea of removing emotion and not needing to sit in front of charts all day. At first it was exciting, but I ran into the same problems over and over: bugs, instability, rising costs, and a lot more complexity than I expected.

I’m self-taught, so a lot of this has been trial and error. I’ve spent a lot of time building, breaking things, rebuilding, and learning what actually matters versus what just sounds interesting in theory.

The biggest lesson for me has been that it’s way better to build one solid system slowly than to keep chasing new ideas and stacking on more moving parts. That was probably my biggest mistake early on.

More recently I shifted away from crypto and started focusing on a more structured day trading approach. It’s felt like a better environment for testing because things are a little less chaotic and easier to work with.

This week I hit a milestone that felt really good: I got my system to place its first paper trade on a simulated funded setup. It’s still a work in progress and I’m debugging things every day, but it feels good to finally see it functioning in a more real workflow.

It definitely hasn’t been smooth, but I’ve learned a lot through the process. Mostly I just wanted to post this for anyone else out there trying to build something similar, because progress in this kind of stuff is usually a lot messier than people expect.

comments.author: No-Consequence-1779
comments.body: Be aware of the trends. Everyone one makes gains when the market goes up. Horizontal is where the skill comes in.
